hero.headline: I build {accent:native macOS apps}, {accent:computer-vision systems}, and the occasional {italic:pair of AR glasses}.
hero.intro1: Junior at Bellarmine College Prep. Co-founder and engineering lead of {link:/products/hyperform-fitness:Hyperform Fitness}, a CV platform that watches a lifter mid-set and corrects their form before the rep is over — over a million reps analyzed across pilot installations.
hero.intro2: Under {strong:ChargerTools LLC} I ship native Mac apps — {link:/products/meridian:Meridian}, {link:/products/probe:Probe}, {link:/products/zenith:Zenith}, {link:/products/futz:Futz} — and tinker with wearable AR, optics, and RF on the side.

about.quote: I'm Kaden MacLean — a junior at Bellarmine College Prep building at the intersection of hardware, software, and human movement.
bio: I co-founded Hyperform Fitness, a computer-vision startup that tracks human movement in real time and delivers corrective feedback to athletes during their actual sets — not minutes later in a video review, but right then, while the bar is still on their back. We've analyzed over a million reps across gyms, rehab centers, and sports medicine programs.

I also run ChargerTools LLC, where I build native macOS applications and dive into hardware projects: ChargerAgent (a Swift AI agent with real tool use), Charger Mail (a privacy-first email client with local AI), Meridian (a calm, keyboard-first life-OS), Probe (a notch-anchored class copilot that runs entirely on-device), and a series of physics simulators that started as research tools for my AR glasses prototype.

I'm on FRC Team 254 (The Cheesy Poofs), where I work in manufacturing, assembly & wiring, and serve as Human Player. I was selected to the FIRST Hall of Fame Student Advisory Council and represent Team 254 for 2025–2026. I previously captained a CyberPatriot team to National Semi-Finalist in Linux security competition, and was an invited speaker at Georgetown's NSLC on nuclear-weapons threats to homeland security.

When I'm not in front of a screen or a lathe, I'm playing piano, cello, viola, or bass — principal bassist of BCP Chamber Orchestra and a classical pianist since I was four. There's a thread between practicing a Chopin etude and debugging a pose-estimation pipeline that I find hard to articulate but easy to feel.
about.pullQuote: The discipline carries over into engineering — both require obsession with detail, deliberate practice, and the patience to slow down on the parts that aren't working yet.

items.name: Python
items.description: General-purpose scripting, data work, and physics simulators.
items.whereLearned: Self-taught starting around 2021; sharpened through computer-vision experiments at Hyperform and the optics / RF radar simulators.

items.name: Swift
items.description: Apple's modern systems language — the daily driver for native macOS work.
items.whereLearned: Self-taught while building Meridian, Probe, ChargerAgent, and Charger Mail at ChargerTools.

items.name: TypeScript
items.description: Typed JavaScript for the web — server and client.
items.whereLearned: Self-taught while building this site, the Hyperform admin dashboard, and internal tooling.

items.name: JavaScript
items.description: The browser runtime — DOM, async, frontend logic.

about.cta: Want to collaborate, ask a question, or just chat? I'm open to hardware/software collaborations, technical advising, speaking, and the kind of email that starts with "this is going to sound dumb but…"

now.intro: A snapshot of what's on my workbench, my desk, and my mind. Inspired by Derek Sivers' /now movement. I update this when something meaningfully changes.

items.title: Meridian
items.body: Native macOS life-OS — calendar, tasks, Canvas LMS, all in one keyboard-first surface. The unified CalendarFeed architecture is finally solid; now I'm focused on the planning engine's slot-finder and getting the recurrence expander 100% deterministic.

items.title: Probe
items.body: Notch-anchored class copilot. The notch UI works; the prompt engineering for fidelity across five note styles is where the time is going. Small local LLMs love to fabricate structure, and I'm building anti-fabrication heuristics into every template.

items.title: Hyperform Fitness
items.body: Edge-first computer-vision platform we co-founded. Past 1M reps. Currently focused on multi-camera sync improvements and pushing toward real-time corrective feedback at sub-100ms across more lift variations.

items.title: Antenna theory & Maxwell's equations
items.body: For the next iteration of the RF radar simulator — finally trying to understand the math under the propagation engine I built two years ago.

items.title: The Mythical Man-Month
items.body: Re-reading after a year of managing interns at Hyperform. Lots of "oh that's why" moments.

items.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
items.body: Background reading for the Meridian sync layer.

items.title: Bach — Cello Suite No. 1, Prelude
items.body: Practicing this on cello and piano simultaneously. They make different sense in each hand.

items.title: Bon Iver — SABLE,
items.body: On heavy rotation in the workshop.

items.title: Glove chord input
items.body: Capacitive touch + ESP32, mapped to a stenotype-style chord set for the AR glasses.

items.title: Rebuilding my workshop
items.body: Better dust collection on the lathe, a proper soldering station, and a CNC fixture plate that doesn't wobble.

contact.intro: Have a project idea, technical question, or just want to say hi? Send a message — I read everything and respond within 24–48 hours.

homepage.closerHeadline: The fastest way to make me smile is an {accent:interesting email}.
homepage.closerBody: I read every message. Hardware, computer vision, native macOS, what it's like to run a startup in high school — let's talk.
homepage.newsletterBody: Occasional notes when I ship something new. No noise, no schedule.

footer.tagline: Building under ChargerTools LLC in Soquel, CA. Usually at a workbench, a lathe, or a lifter's squat rack; occasionally at a piano.
